Security 9. Retention 10. Your rights 11. Marketing 12. Cookies and local storage 13. Children 14. Security compromises 15. Changes 16. Contact and complaints

Kitchen Cost Pro Legal

Privacy Policy

This Privacy Policy explains how Kitchen Cost Pro collects, uses, stores, shares, and protects personal information when you use the KCP platform, website, integrations, support channels, and related services.

Effective: 14 July 2026 Version: 2026-07-14 South Africa

1. Scope and responsible party

Kitchen Cost Pro, also referred to as KCP, is a business operations platform for food-service businesses. The KCP service provider identified in your order form, subscription agreement, invoice, or onboarding communication is the responsible party for personal information processed for its own business purposes.

Your employer, restaurant group, franchise, or other organisation may also be a responsible party for information entered into its KCP workspace. Where KCP processes information only on that organisation's instructions, KCP acts as an operator for that organisation.

This policy is intended to support compliance with the Protection of Personal Information Act 4 of 2013, commonly called POPIA, and other applicable South African laws.

2. Information we process

Depending on how you use KCP, we may process the following categories of information:

  • Identity and contact information: name, business email address, telephone number, role, employer, and workspace membership.
  • Account and security information: account identifiers, password hashes, authentication events, reset requests, session information, permissions, and security challenge results.
  • Business and workspace information: business name, locations, suppliers, users, recipes, stock items, purchasing records, sales mappings, operational records, and configuration settings.
  • Integration information: identifiers, catalogue data, order data, refund data, location data, webhook events, and access tokens from connected services such as point-of-sale, email, accounting, or storage providers.
  • Technical and usage information: IP address, browser and device information, timestamps, request logs, error logs, audit logs, feature usage, and diagnostic information.
  • Support information: messages, screenshots, files, feedback, and communications submitted to KCP support or administrators.
  • Billing and commercial information: subscription plan, invoices, payment status, and related account records. Payment card details may be processed directly by a payment service provider rather than stored by KCP.

3. Sources of information

We obtain information from:

  • you when you register, sign in, configure a workspace, contact support, or use the service;
  • your organisation, workspace administrators, managers, or authorised users;
  • connected third-party services and integrations that you or your organisation authorise;
  • automated technical systems, including logs, cookies, local storage, and security tools; and
  • public or commercial sources where lawful and reasonably necessary for account administration, fraud prevention, or business verification.

4. Why we process information

We process personal information for legitimate and lawful purposes, including to:

  • create and administer accounts, workspaces, permissions, and user access;
  • provide stock, recipe costing, purchasing, manufacturing, transfer, sales integration, reporting, scheduling, and related features;
  • connect to authorised third-party services and process integration events;
  • authenticate users, prevent fraud, detect abuse, investigate incidents, and protect the service;
  • provide support, maintain the platform, diagnose errors, and improve reliability;
  • send operational notices, security notices, reports, invitations, password resets, and service communications;
  • manage subscriptions, invoicing, payment status, and contractual obligations;
  • comply with legal duties, enforce agreements, and establish or defend legal claims; and
  • analyse aggregated or de-identified information to improve KCP products and services.

We rely on consent where consent is required, performance of a contract, compliance with legal obligations, protection of legitimate interests, and other lawful grounds permitted by POPIA.

5. Required information and consequences

Some information is required to create an account, secure the platform, provide contracted services, or comply with law. If you do not provide required information, we may be unable to create an account, activate a workspace, complete an integration, provide a feature, or respond to a request.

Information marked as optional may be withheld, but this may reduce the usefulness or accuracy of certain features, reports, or workflows.

6. Sharing and operators

We may share information with:

  • authorised users and administrators within your organisation or linked business group;
  • hosting, infrastructure, security, email, support, analytics, storage, and professional service providers that assist KCP;
  • third-party integrations that your organisation chooses to connect;
  • auditors, accountants, legal advisers, insurers, and other professional advisers where reasonably necessary;
  • law enforcement, regulators, courts, or public authorities where required or permitted by law; and
  • a successor or purchaser in connection with a lawful merger, restructuring, financing, or sale of all or part of the business.

Operators are required to process information only for authorised purposes and to apply appropriate security measures. KCP does not sell personal information to advertisers.

7. Cross-border processing

KCP and its service providers may process or store information outside South Africa. Where information is transferred across borders, we take reasonable steps to ensure that the recipient is subject to a law, agreement, corporate rule, or other arrangement that provides an adequate level of protection as required by POPIA.

8. Security

We use reasonable technical and organisational safeguards designed to protect personal information against loss, unauthorised access, interference, alteration, disclosure, or destruction. Measures may include access controls, authentication, encryption in transit, logging, tenant separation, least-privilege access, secure development practices, backups, and incident response procedures.

No internet service can guarantee absolute security. You are responsible for using strong passwords, protecting account credentials, limiting access to authorised users, and promptly reporting suspected compromise.

9. Retention

We retain personal information for as long as reasonably necessary to provide the service, meet contractual and legal obligations, resolve disputes, maintain audit trails, protect against fraud, and enforce agreements. Retention periods depend on the type of information, the purpose of processing, legal requirements, workspace configuration, and whether an account remains active.

Information may be deleted, anonymised, or securely isolated when it is no longer required. Backup copies may remain for a limited period until overwritten through normal backup cycles.

10. Your rights

Subject to POPIA and applicable exceptions, you may have the right to:

  • ask whether we hold personal information about you;
  • request access to your personal information;
  • request correction, updating, deletion, or destruction of inaccurate, excessive, outdated, incomplete, misleading, or unlawfully obtained information;
  • object to processing in appropriate circumstances;
  • withdraw consent where processing is based on consent;
  • object to direct marketing; and
  • submit a complaint to the Information Regulator.

We may need to verify your identity before acting on a request. Where your organisation controls the relevant workspace information, we may refer your request to that organisation or require its instructions.

11. Marketing communications

We may send marketing communications only where permitted by law. You may opt out using the unsubscribe method in the communication or by contacting KCP. Opting out of marketing does not stop essential service, security, billing, legal, or operational communications.

12. Cookies and local storage

KCP may use cookies, browser storage, and similar technologies to maintain sessions, remember preferences, improve security, support authentication, and operate the service. Disabling required storage may prevent sign-in or other essential functionality.

13. Children

KCP is intended for business users and is not directed to children. A person who is not legally able to enter into a binding agreement may use KCP only through an authorised organisation and with any consent required by law.

14. Security compromises

If we have reasonable grounds to believe that personal information has been accessed or acquired by an unauthorised person, we will investigate and provide notifications to affected responsible parties, data subjects, and the Information Regulator where required by law.

15. Changes to this policy

We may update this policy to reflect changes to the service, law, security practices, or business operations. The updated version will display a new effective date and version number. We may provide additional notice where a change is material.

16. Contact and complaints

Privacy requests and legal notices should be sent through the KCP support contact identified in your account, order form, subscription agreement, invoice, or onboarding communication. Please include your name, organisation, workspace, contact details, and a clear description of the request.

Information Regulator South Africa

You may also submit a complaint to the Information Regulator. Current forms and contact information are available at inforegulator.org.za.

Kitchen Cost Pro Privacy Policy, version 2026-07-14. This policy should be read with the Terms of Service and any signed service agreement.
